docs.title: Hako-Core 4U Server Chassis
docs.text: Welcome to the official documentation for the Hako-Core Server Chassis. This comprehensive guide will help you understand, install, and maintain your Hako-Core system.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Check the Quick Start guide that came packaged with your system. Head over to the Get Started page to get help with the rest of your set up.

docs.title: What is the Hako-Core
docs.text:
The Hako-Core 4U Server Chassis is designed for high-density storage applications with support for:

  • Multiple motherboard formats: Mini-ITX, Micro ATX, ATX, EATX
  • Flexible storage options: SSDs, HDDs, and mixed configurations with up to 48 hard drives.
  • Cooling: Support for up to 9 fans total along with accessory PCIe dedicated fans
  • Modular design: Expandable with additional cages and PCBs

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
For questions, support, or warranty issues:

  • Email: help@hakoforge.com
  • GitHub: All documentation versions available on our GitHub repository
  • Warranty: 90-day limited warranty included

The Hako-Core will be an ongoing project. As new cages or PCBs are made, this manual may be updated or changed.

docs.title: Hako-Core Mini
docs.text:
Left and Right Rails

Take note of the L and R stickers on the rails and mount them on the correct side.

Inner Server Rail Mounting Holes

When installing the inner server rails, use the screw holes shown in the diagram. Not all holes in the server rail are used.

docs.title: Server Rack Ears
docs.text:
These metal flanges are used to secure the Hako-Core to a server rack's frame.

Left and Right Server Rack Ears

Notice the rack frame holes are along the bottom part of the rack ears. Use this feature to distinguish between left and right rack ears.

Supporting The Chassis In A Server Rack

The rack ears alone are not enough to hold the chassis in a server rack. Server rails must be used. Rack ears are only to prevent the chassis from sliding out of the rack.

docs.title: Fan Installation
docs.text:
Use 2 of the mounting holes to secure a fan to the angled bracket.

Direction of Air Flow

Take note of the fan mount direction to ensure proper air flow. Depending on the bracket orientation installed in the case, the fan may be flipped.

docs.title: Overhang Mount
docs.text:
The mounting location can be moved left and right as well as up and down.

Lower Mount Point

When installing in the lowered position, one of the mount points will not be used.

docs.title: Underhang Mount
docs.text: The angled fan bracket can also be under mounted. This can be useful depending on wire clearances with different HBA cards.

docs.title: Mount Extension
docs.text: The included mount extensions can be used to extend the bracket out for extra coverage on the furthest PCIe card. Use the extended screws for this configuration.

docs.title: Fan Installation
docs.text:
Use 4 of the mounting holes to secure a fan to the horizontal bracket.

Direction of Air Flow

Take note of the fan mount direction to ensure proper air flow. Depending on the bracket orientation installed in the case, the fan may be flipped.

docs.title: Overhang Mount
docs.text:
The mounting location can be moved left and right as well as up and down.

Left or Right Mount Points

When installing in the shifted left or right position, one of the mount points will not be used.

docs.title: Safety & Cautions
docs.text:
Important Safety Information

The Hako-Core includes a powerboard that will be connected to a power supply. Please read all safety information carefully before proceeding.

docs.title: Power Supply Considerations
docs.text:
The Hako-Core powerboard connects directly to your power supply. Always follow proper electrical safety procedures:

  • Ensure your power supply is disconnected and unpowered before making any connections
  • Verify all connections are secure before powering on
  • Refer to the Powerboard section for detailed connection information

docs.title: Connector Safety
docs.text:
To protect yourself and electronic components:

  • Keep unused connectors clear from contacting other surfaces
  • Ensure proper PSU connectors are used (refer to the quick start guide)
  • Never force connections - they should fit easily when properly aligned

docs.title: Before You Begin
docs.text:
1. Power down and disconnect all equipment
  2. Ground yourself to prevent static discharge
  3. Prepare your workspace with adequate lighting
  4. Have proper tools ready (screwdrivers, cable ties, etc.)

docs.title: During Installation
docs.text:
• Handle components carefully - avoid dropping or striking parts
  • Support heavy components when installing drives or PCBs or GPUs
  • Route cables properly to avoid pinching or interference
  • Check for interference before closing panels

docs.title: Component Handling
docs.text:
Best Practices

  • Handle PCBs by their edges only
  • Avoid touching connectors with your fingers
  • Store removed components in anti-static bags when possible
  • Keep your workspace clean and organized

docs.title: What NOT to Do
docs.text:
Avoid These Common Mistakes

  • Don't work on powered equipment
  • Don't use excessive force on connections
  • Don't mix up power connections from different rails
  • Don't bend or twist PCBs
  • Don't skip grounding procedures

docs.title: Emergency Procedures
docs.text:
If you encounter any issues during installation:

  1. Immediately disconnect power if you smell burning or see sparks
  2. Stop work if you're unsure about any connection
  3. Contact support at help@hakoforge.com

docs.title: Motherboard
docs.text: The Hako-Core supports anything from a Mini-ITX up to an E-ATX.

docs.title: Hako-Core Mini With E-ATX
docs.text: The Hako-Core Mini when using an E-ATX motherboard limits the second row of backplane to only the small backplane

docs.title: Power Supply
docs.text: Use the respective mounts for your specific power supply. The Hako-Core supports up to 200mm power supply.

docs.title: Mini-SAS Zip Tie Colors
docs.text:
The Mini-SAS cables have zip ties on them. Each color represents the row of backplanes it is connected to. The number of zip ties represent which backplane in that row it is connected to.

  • Row 1: Yellow
  • Row 2: Red
  • Row 3: Blue

docs.title: MOBO FAN CTL Position
docs.text: With the jumper in this position, all fan headers will be controlled by the PWM_IN connector. This can be connected to a header on your motherboard or a dedicated fan controller. The USB connection is not required.

docs.title: POWERBOARD FAN CTL Position
docs.text: With the jumper in this position, all fan headers will be controlled by the powerboard. A USB connection is required. With the Hako Foundry software, you're able to control each fan wall individually.

docs.title: 8-pin PCIe Connectors
docs.text: Each 8-Pin PCIe connector powers a specific set of hardware. If there is a set of hardware that is not being used in your configuration, connection is optional.

docs.title: 8-Pin PCIe IN 1
docs.text:
• Row 1* Backplane 4
  • Row 1* Backplane 3
  • Fan wall 1

docs.title: 8-Pin PCIe IN 2
docs.text:
• Row 1* Backplane 2
  • Row 1* Backplane 1

docs.title: 8-Pin PCIe IN 3
docs.text:
• Row 2* Backplane 4
  • Row 2* Backplane 3
  • Fan wall 2

docs.title: 8-Pin PCIe IN 4
docs.text:
• Row 2* Backplane 2
  • Row 2* Backplane 1
  • Fan wall 3**

Dual Powerboard Hako-Core Configuration

* Row 1 and Row 2 connections on the second powerboard are connected to Row 3 and Row 4 ** The exposed Fan Wall 3 headers can be used for other fans if there is no fan wall 3 in your configuration

docs.title: Mini-B USB to USB 2.0 Header
docs.text:
The Powerboard comes with a Mini-B USB to USB 2.0 Header that will connect to a USB 2.0 Header on your motherboard. This connection is required for individual fan wall control and power monitoring of drives. If these features are not used, the USB connection is optional.

USB 2.0 Header Connector Orientation

Take note of the empty pin on the connector and align them with the motherboard properly. Improper connection may cause damage to components.

docs.title: PWM_IN to Fan Header
docs.text:
The Powerboard comes with a PWM_IN to USB 2.0 Header that will connect to a fan header on your motherboard. The connected motherboard fan header will control all fans connected to the powerboard. If fan control is not desired, leaving this unplugged will have the fans run at full speed.

PWM_IN and USB connected

Having both the PWM_IN and USB wires connected to the motherboard is OK. This configuration will allow you to switch between fan controls with the jumper.

docs.title: Chassis Fans
docs.text:
Fan Thickness Compatibility

Only Fan Wall 1 and Fan wall 3 are capable of 35mm thickness fans. Fan Wall 2 is only able to fit 25mm thickness fans.

docs.title: Fan Wall 1
docs.text:
Remove the magnetic faceplate and install the fans using the mounting holes.

Air Block Clearance

The 3D printed air blocks may be too tight for fan installation. Unscrew these and reinstall them along with the fan at the same time to get them to fit together.

docs.title: Fan Headers
docs.text: The fan headers for Fan Wall 1 are located above the the left fan and connect to the powerboard. Plug the fans in here and tuck the wires in between the fans.

docs.title: Fan Wall 2/3
docs.text:
Middle Fan Headers

Fan Wall 2 uses fan extension cable to connect to the powerboard. Plugging the fans in first and tucking the wires under the fan makes the install easier.

Install the fans using the mounting holes. Fan wall 2 and 3 use similar mounting points. The fourth screw on the edge fans are not used.

docs.title: Fan Extension
docs.text: This cable is used for Fan Wall 2.

docs.title: Exposed Powerboard Headers
docs.text:
Fan Wall 3 uses the exposed fan headers on the powerboard.

Hako-Core Mini Fan Walls

The Hako-Core Mini does not utilize a third fan wall so the exposed headers can be used for any other fans you may have.

docs.title: Exhaust Fan
docs.text: Use the 2 mount points to install a 80 mm or 92 mm fan to help facilitate airflow.

docs.title: Accessory Fans
docs.text: See Accessory Fan Bracket Mounts to get instructions on the accessory bracket installation.

docs.title: Hako Foundry
docs.text: The Hako Foundry is the companion Docker app that is available on our GitHub. Check the README there for a full explanation.

docs.title: Physical Dimensions
docs.text: Dimension Measurement Length 34.0 in (863.6mm) Width 17.25 in (438.15mm) Height 6.875 in (174.6mm)

docs.title: Storage Capacity
docs.text:
• 2.5" SSD/HDD: Up to 36 drives (with 12-SSD backplanes)
  • 3.5" HDD: Up to 36 drives (with 4-HDD backplanes)
  • Mixed Configuration: Combination of 2.5" and 3.5" drives

docs.title: Weight Specifications
docs.text: Configuration Weight Minimal Setup 20 lbs 3 fans, 3 cages & PCBs, no drives Standard Setup 25 lbs 3 fans, 9 cages & PCBs, no drives Fully Loaded ~35 to ~90 lbs 3 fans, 9 cages & PCBs, 42 HDD, 6 SSD (depending on drives used)

docs.title: Drive Power Capability
docs.text: Configuration Power Output Single Powerboard 600 Watts Dual Powerboards 1,200 Watts

docs.title: Motherboard Support
docs.text:
The Hako-Core supports a wide range of motherboard form factors:

  • Mini-ITX
  • Micro ATX
  • ATX
  • EATX (Extended ATX)

docs.title: Fan Support
docs.text: Location Quantity Notes Included Fans 3 fans Pre-installed in chassis Additional Front Up to 3 more Requires removal of front drive cages Additional Rear/Middle Up to 6 more Requires removal of respective drive cages Total Maximum 9 fans With appropriate cage removal

docs.title: Power Supply Support
docs.text:
• Form Factor: ATX standard
  • Maximum Length: 200mm
  • Connector Requirements: Standard ATX power connectors
  • Recommended Wattage: 750W+ for full configuration

docs.title: Airflow Design
docs.text:
• Front-to-rear airflow pattern
  • Positive pressure configuration recommended
  • Variable speed control through motherboard or fan controller

docs.title: Materials
docs.text:
• Construction: Powder Coated Aluminum
  • Finish: Durable powder coating for long-lasting protection
  • Mounting: Standard rack-mountable 4U form factor

docs.title: Hot-Swap Support
docs.text:
• All drive bays support hot-swappable operation
  • SAS/SATA compatibility across all bays
  • Individual drive power and data connectivity

docs.title: Warranty
docs.text:
All HakoForge products come with a 90-day limited warranty.

Contact us at help@hakoforge.com for warranty support.

This warranty is limited to the original purchaser and warrants that the product is free from manufacturing defects for the specified time period.

This warranty does not cover damage or problems caused by, but not limited to:

  • Wear and tear from normal use
  • Any improper use that does not follow procedures described in the manual
  • Any accidents or modifications
  • Incorrect use with a power supply

HakoForge recommends regular backups and proper data storage practices. HakoForge is not responsible for any data loss that may occur.

docs.title: Contact Information
docs.text: Warranty Claims: help@hakoforge.com Response Time: 24-48 hours typical Business Hours: Monday-Friday, 9 AM - 5 PM PST

docs.title: Required Information
docs.text:
When contacting warranty support, please provide:

  1. Product information

    • Model number and version
    • Serial number (if applicable)

  2. Problem description

    • Detailed symptoms observed

  3. Purchase documentation

    • Original receipt or invoice

HakoForge will, at its discretion:

  1. Repair the defective product
  2. Replace with new or refurbished equivalent
  3. Refund the purchase price (less shipping)

docs.title: Welcome to the Forge!
docs.text: Thank you for supporting HakoForge! This user manual will guide you through the installation and usage of the Hako-Core chassis components and products.

docs.title: About This Manual
docs.text:
Please read this user manual carefully before use. It covers:

  • Installation procedures for all components
  • Safety guidelines and best practices
  • Troubleshooting and maintenance tips
  • Warranty and support information

docs.title: Pre-assembled vs Kit Options
docs.text:
• If the Hako-Core was configured at purchase, it will come pre-assembled and ready for use.
  • If backplane kits are bought to be placed in an existing Hako-Core, refer to the Backplane installation page.

docs.title: Living Documentation
docs.text:
The Hako-Core is an ongoing project with continuous improvements. As new cages or PCBs are developed:

  • This manual will be updated accordingly.
  • All versions and documentation are available on our GitHub repository.
  • You'll be notified of significant updates via our email subscriber list or our discord.

docs.title: Support
docs.text: For any questions, technical support, or warranty issues: Email: help@hakoforge.com

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Standard 12-SSD Backplane provides high-density 2.5" drive storage with integrated power regulation and SAS/SATA connectivity for up to 12 solid-state drives or 2.5" hard drives.

This backplane is designed for maximum 2.5" drive density while providing clean power delivery and flexible connectivity options.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• 12 SATA/SAS 2.5" drive connectors for maximum storage density
  • Integrated 5V power regulation from 12V input
  • Triple SAS output connectors for flexible HBA connectivity
  • Hot-swap capability with proper drive cages
  • LED activity indicators for each drive bay

docs.title: Technical Specifications
docs.text: Parameter Specification Drive Connectors 12 x SATA/SAS 2.5" SAS Outputs 3 SAS Out Connectors Input Voltage 12V DC Output Voltage 5V (25A Max) Max Power Consumption 84 Watts Hot-Swap Yes

docs.title: Status Indicators
docs.text:
Each drive bay includes: - Power LED Green when drive is powered

Power LED Compatibility

Some drives do not trigger the LED but are functioning correctly.

docs.title: Installation Guide
docs.text: For PCB and Backplane Installation see PCB Installation For Drive Cage Installation see Cage Installation

docs.title: Drive Installation
docs.text:
1. Insert drives gently into drive cages.
  2. Gently press drive down until firmly seated.
  3. Verify with LED indicators after system power-on.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Standard 4-HDD Backplane provides power and data connectivity for up to four 3.5" hard disk drives or large-capacity SSDs, with integrated power regulation and SAS connectivity.

This backplane is optimized for traditional 3.5" hard disk drives, providing the higher power requirements and robust connectivity needed for spinning disk storage systems.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• 12 SATA/SAS 2.5" drive connectors for maximum storage density
  • Integrated 5V power regulation from 12V input
  • Triple SAS output connectors for flexible HBA connectivity
  • Hot-swap capability with proper drive cages
  • LED activity indicators for each drive bay

docs.title: Technical Specifications
docs.text: Parameter Specification Drive Connectors 4 x SATA/SAS 3.5" SAS Output 1 SAS Out Connector Input Voltage 12V DC Output Voltages 12V pass-through, 5V regulated (5A Max) Max Power Consumption 53 Watts Hot-Swap Yes

docs.title: Status Indicators
docs.text:
Each drive bay includes: - Power LED Green when drive is powered - Activity LED Flashing during I/O operations

Power LED Compatibility

Some drives do not trigger the LEDs but are functioning correctly.

docs.title: Installation Guide
docs.text: For PCB and Backplane Installation see PCB Installation For Drive Cage Installation see Cage Installation

docs.title: Drive Installation
docs.text:
1. Insert drives gently into drive cages.
  2. Gently press drive down until firmly seated.
  3. Verify with LED indicators after system power-on.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Mixed 4-Bay Backplane provides the flexibility of both 2.5" and 3.5" drive support in a single PCB, ideal for hybrid storage configurations combining SSDs and traditional hard drives.

This backplane combines the best of both storage worlds, allowing users to optimize for both performance (SSD) and capacity (HDD) within the same chassis row.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Hybrid connectivity 2x 2.5" SSD + 2x 3.5" HDD connectors
  • Unified power management for both drive types

docs.title: Technical Specifications
docs.text: Parameter Specification 2.5" Connectors 2 x SATA/SAS 2.5" 3.5" Connectors 2 x SATA/SAS 3.5" SAS Output 1 SAS Out Connector Input Voltage 12V DC Output Voltages 12V pass-through, 5V regulated (5A Max) Max Power Consumption 40 Watts Hot-Swap Yes

docs.title: Status Indicators
docs.text:
Each drive bay includes: - Power LED Green when drive is powered - Activity LED Flashing during I/O operations

Power LED Compatibility

Some drives do not trigger the LEDs but are functioning correctly.

docs.title: Installation Guide
docs.text: For PCB and Backplane Installation see PCB Installation For Drive Cage Installation see Cage Installation

docs.title: Drive Installation
docs.text:
1. Insert drives gently into drive cages.
  2. Gently press drive down until firmly seated.
  3. Verify with LED indicators after system power-on.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Drive cages provide the mechanical interface between storage devices and the Hako-Core chassis. They enable hot-swappable operation and ensure proper alignment with PCB connectors.

The Hako-Core supports multiple cage types to accommodate different storage configurations. Each cage is designed for specific drive formats and mounting requirements.

docs.title: Cage Types
docs.text: Cage Type Drive Format Capacity Hot-Swap 12-SSD Cage 2.5" SSD 12 drives Yes 4-HDD Cage 3.5" HDD 4 drives Yes Mixed Cage 2.5" + 3.5" 2+2 drives Yes

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Tool-free installation using thumb screws
  • Keyed alignment prevents incorrect mounting
  • Hot-swap carriers for individual drive access
  • Airflow channels for optimal cooling

docs.title: Installing the Drive Cage
docs.text:
Safety First

It is always recommended to power down the system when working inside the case.

For how to remove the lid see Lid Removal

  1. Power down the system completely
  2. Remove chassis lid following panel removal procedure
  3. Identify target mounting bracket (rear or middle)
  4. Orient cage correctly - connectors toward PCB
  5. Align cage tabs with bracket mounting slots
  6. Slide cage into position against the bracket
  7. Ensure flush contact across all mounting points
  8. Install the thumb screws through cage tabs into cage bracket and tighten snugly.

This procedure works for all 3 cage types (SSD, HDD, Mixed)

docs.title: Exploded View
docs.text: Understanding the Hako-Core chassis components and their assembly relationships is crucial for proper installation and maintenance.

docs.title: Component Identification
docs.text: The Hako-Core Server Chassis consists of 9 main components that assemble together to form the complete chassis system.

docs.title: Primary Components
docs.text: Item Component Name Function Assembly Notes 1 PSU Bracket Mounts ATX power supply Rear-mounted, supports up to 210mm PSU 2 PCIe I/O Bracket Motherboard I/O support Aligns with motherboard rear panel 3 Chassis Body Main structure housing Contains all internal components 4 Chassis Lid Removable access panel Slide-and-lift mechanism 5 Rear Cage Bracket Supports rear drive cages Modular mounting system 6 Front Cage Bracket Supports middle drive cages Optional fan mounting 7 Top Front Fan Bracket Upper front fan mounting Supports 3x 120mm fans 8 Bottom Front Fan Bracket Lower front fan mounting Intake configuration 9 Front Panel Magnetic access panel Tool-free removal

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Hako-Core supports up to 9 fans
  • Hako-Core Mini supports up to 6 fans

docs.title: Hako-Core
docs.text: Location Included Additional Total Front Panel 3 fans 0 fans 3 fans Middle Cage Bracket 0 fans +3 fans 3 fans Rear Cage Bracket 0 fans +3 fans 3 fans Grand Total 3 fans +6 fans 9 fans

docs.title: Hako-Core Mini
docs.text: Location Included Additional Total Front Panel 3 fans 0 fans 3 fans Middle Cage Bracket 0 fans +3 fans 3 fans Rear Cage Bracket 0 fans +3 fans 3 fans Grand Total 3 fans +6 fans 9 fans

docs.title: Installation Process
docs.text:
Cage Removal Required

There must be no drive cages installed in the first row to access fan mounting points.

  1. Locate the magnetic attachment points on the front panel
  2. Gently pull the panel away from the chassis - magnets will release
  3. Use provided screws to secure the fans onto the mounting locations.
  4. Connect fan power and control cables

docs.title: Installation Process
docs.text:
Cage Clearance

There must be no cages installed around the respective rows for fan access. Remove cages before attempting fan installation.

  1. Remove relevant drive cages from the target row
  2. Mount fans in correct orientation (airflow pointing out the back of the chassis)
  3. Secure with provided screws
  4. Connect fan power and control cables

docs.title: Fan Power Options
docs.text:
1. Hako-Core Powerboard

    • 4-pin fan outputs available
    • Powered by PCIe IN 1 rail
    • Up to 4 fans per powerboard

  2. Hako-Core Mini Powerboard

    • 4-pin fan outputs available
    • Powered by PCIe IN 1 rail
    • Up to 4 fans per powerboard

  3. Dedicated Fan Controller

    • External PWM controller
    • Advanced temperature-based control
    • Support for many fans

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Safe and proper removal of chassis lid is essential for accessing internal components.

The Hako-Core chassis lid is designed for quick, tool-minimal removal while maintaining structural integrity when closed. The Hako-Core features a slide-and-lift lid mechanism for easy maintenance access.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Slide-and-lift design for easy removal.
  • No special tools required beyond phillips head screwdriver.

docs.title: Basic Tools
docs.text: • Phillips #2 head screwdriver (magnetic tip recommended).

docs.title: Optional Tools
docs.text:
• Magnetic parts tray for screw storage.
  • Clean cloth for handling.

Safety First

It is always recommended to power down the system when working inside the case.

Warning!

Check that no cables will be pinched or stressed during lid removal or installation. Do not overtighten to avoid stripping the screw. There is 1 extra screw provided in the "extras bag".

docs.title: Removing the Front Lid
docs.text:
1. Locate the lid screw on the top front edge of the chassis.
  2. Use a #2 Phillips head screwdriver to remove the screw.
  3. Slide the lid towards the front of the case to unlatch.
  4. Lift and remove the lid

docs.title: Removing the rear Lid
docs.text:
1. Locate the lid screws on the left and right side of the chassis and use a #2 Phillips head screwdriver to remove the screws.
  2. Locate the thumb screw on the back of the chassis and use a #2 Phillips head screwdriver to remove the screw.
  3. Slide the lid towards the rear of the case to unlatch.
  4. Lift and remove the lid.

docs.title: Reassembling the rear lid
docs.text:
1. Lower lid onto chassis.
  2. Slide lid towards the front of the chassis.
  3. Reinstall the 2 screws on the right and left side of the chassis and tighten snugly.
  4. Reinstall the thumb screw on the back of the chassis and tighten snugly.

docs.title: Reassembling the front lid
docs.text:
1. Lower lid onto chassis.
  2. Slide lid toward the rear of the chassis.
  3. Reinstall the single screw on the top front edge of the chassis and tighten snugly.

docs.title: Removing the Lid
docs.text:
1. Locate the lid screw on the top front edge of the chassis.
  2. Use a #2 Phillips head screwdriver to remove the screw.
  3. Slide the lid towards the rear of the case to unlatch.
  4. Lift and remove the lid.

docs.title: Reassembling the lid
docs.text:
1. Lower lid onto chassis.
  2. Slide lid toward the front of the chassis.
  3. Reinstall the securing screw and tighten snugly.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
PCB installation is a critical step in setting up your Hako-Core or Mini chassis. This guide covers the proper mounting procedures for all backplane types and ensures secure, reliable connections.

Each PCB in the Hako-Core system provides power regulation and data connectivity for a specific set of drives. Proper installation ensures optimal performance and system reliability.

docs.title: PCB Types
docs.text:
The Hako-Core and Hako-Core Mini supports several PCB configurations:

  • 12-SSD Backplane: 2.5" drive support
  • 4-HDD Backplane: 3.5" drive support
  • Mixed 4-Bay Backplane: Combined 2.5" and 3.5" support

docs.title: Basic Tools
docs.text: • Phillips #2 head screwdriver (magnetic tip recommended).

docs.title: Optional Tools
docs.text:
• Magnetic parts tray for screw storage.
  • Clean cloth for handling.
  • Anti-static wrist strap
  • Proper lighting for clear visibility

docs.title: Installation Procedure
docs.text:
Safety First

It is always recommended to power down the system when working inside the case.

docs.title: Step 1: Identify Mounting Location
docs.text:
Each PCB has designated mounting locations within the chassis:

  1. Locate standoffs in chassis for your PCB position
  2. Align PCB over the mounting standoffs in correct orientation
  3. Verify all mounting holes align with standoffs
  4. Secure PCB with provided screws. (Recommended to start opposite corners first)

Torque Specification

Do not over-tighten screws. PCBs can crack under excessive pressure. Tighten just until snug with no movement.

docs.title: Visual Inspection
docs.text:
After installation, verify:

  • PCB sits level and secure
  • All screws properly tightened
  • No visible cable stress or interference
  • Any connectors are fully seated

docs.title: Electrical Testing
docs.text:
Before installing drives:

  1. Power on system (without drives)
  2. Check PCB power LEDs if present
  3. Test with single drive before full installation

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Hako-Core power harness system distributes clean, regulated power from the central powerboard to individual PCBs throughout the chassis. This modular approach ensures efficient power delivery and simplified cable management.

The power harness acts as the distribution network between the central powerboard and the individual backplanes.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Modular design with two harness lengths
  • 16-pin to 4-pin distribution architecture
  • Overcurrent protection at the powerboard level

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text: Parameter Specification Input Connector 16-pin keyed connector Output Connectors 4 x 4-pin power connectors Current Rating 150 watts per 8-pin PCIe Voltage 12V DC distribution Length Options Short (18") and Long (36") variants

docs.title: Short Harness (18" / .4cm)
docs.text:
• Use case: PCBs in first two rows from the front of the chasis
  • Quantity needed: 1-8 per system

docs.title: Long Harness (36" / 914mm)
docs.text:
• Use case: PCBs in the 3rd and 4th rows from the front of the chasis
  • Quantity needed: 1-8 per system

docs.title: 16-Pin Input Connector
docs.text: Pin Function Wire Color 1-4 +12V Rail 1 Red 5-8 Ground Rail 1 Black 9-12 +12V Rail 2 Red 13-16 Ground Rail 2 Black

docs.title: 4-Pin Output Connectors
docs.text: Pin Function Wire Color 1 +12V Red 2 Ground Black 3 +12V Red 4 Ground Black

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Safety First

  • Always power down before connecting/disconnecting harnesses
  • Verify proper connector orientation before seating
  • Never force connections - they should fit easily
  • Use proper ESD protection when handling

Cable Management Tips

  • Route longer cables along outer chassis edges
  • Group harnesses by destination area
  • Use color coding for easy identification

docs.title: Step 1: Route Harnesses
docs.text:
1. Plan routing path from powerboard to each PCB
  2. Use appropriate length harness for each connection
  3. Route along chassis edges for longer runs (See Diagram Above)

docs.title: Step 2: Connect to Powerboard
docs.text:
1. Align 16-pin connector with powerboard output
  2. Press firmly until connector fully seats
  3. Verify secure connection with gentle pull test

docs.title: Step 3: Connect to PCBs
docs.text:
1. Install PCBs in their designated locations
  2. Connect 4-pin outputs to appropriate PCB inputs
  3. Press firmly until connector fully seats
  4. Verify secure connection with gentle pull test

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The powerboard is the central power distribution hub for the Hako-Core chassis. It takes 8-pin PCIe power inputs from your ATX power supply and distributes power throughout the Hako-Core power harnesses to individual PCBs.

The powerboard provides clean, regulated power distribution for all storage devices and cooling systems in the Hako-Core chassis.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Input: Multiple 8-pin PCIe power connectors from PSU
  • Output: 24-pin connector to Hako-Core power harnesses
  • Fan Power: Direct 4-pin fan power outputs
  • Modular Design: Support for single or dual powerboard configuration

Power Priority

8-Pin PCIe IN 1 powers the 4-pin fan outputs. This input should be prioritized and connected first to ensure proper fan operation.

docs.title: Hako-Core
docs.text:
Note

The Hako-Core can accept up to 2 powerboards for a maximum power delivery of 1200W total

Before Mounting: Ensure correct orientation of the board.

  1. Mount the powerboard in designated area of chassis (see above image) onto standoffs with the 4 provided screws.

docs.title: Hako-Core Mini
docs.text:
Note

The Hako-Core Mini can only accept a single powerboard.

Before Mounting: Ensure correct orientation of the board.

  1. Mount the powerboard in designated area of chassis (see above image) onto standoffs with the 4 provided screws.

docs.title: Input Connections
docs.text: Each 8-Pin PCIe connector powers a specific set of hardware. If there is a set of hardware that is not being used in your configuration, connection is optional.

docs.title: 8-Pin PCIe IN 1
docs.text:
• Row 1* Backplane 4
  • Row 1* Backplane 3
  • Fan wall 1

docs.title: 8-Pin PCIe IN 2
docs.text:
• Row 1* Backplane 2
  • Row 1* Backplane 1

docs.title: 8-Pin PCIe IN 3
docs.text:
• Row 2* Backplane 4
  • Row 2* Backplane 3
  • Fan wall 2

docs.title: 8-Pin PCIe IN 4
docs.text:
• Row 2* Backplane 2
  • Row 2* Backplane 1
  • Fan wall 3**

Dual Powerboard Hako-Core Configuration

* Row 1 and Row 2 connections on the second powerboard are connected to Row 3 and Row 4 ** The exposed Fan Wall 3 headers can be used for other fans if there is no fan wall 3 in your configuration

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Hako-Core power harness system distributes clean, regulated power from the central powerboard to individual PCBs throughout the chassis. This modular approach ensures efficient power delivery and simplified cable management.

The power harness acts as the distribution network between the central powerboard and the individual backplanes.

docs.title: Short Harness (18" / 45cm)
docs.text:
• Use case: PCBs in first two rows from the front of the chassis
  • Quantity needed: 1-8 per system

docs.title: Medium Harness (24" / 60cm)
docs.text:
• Use case: PCBs in first two rows from the front of the chassis
  • Quantity needed: 1-8 per system

docs.title: Long Harness (36" / 90cm)
docs.text:
• Use case: PCBs in the 3rd and 4th rows from the front of the chassis
  • Quantity needed: 1-8 per system

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Safety First

It is always recommended to power down the system when working inside the case.

docs.title: Installing SAS Cables
docs.text:
1. Plan route from PCB to Motherboard
  2. Use appropriate length SAS cables for each connection
  3. Route along chassis edges for longer runs (See Diagram Above)
  4. Connect SAS cables to PCBs and ensure each cable is snug with a gentle pull test
